file_name: EMERALDHEALTHTHERAPEUTICSINC_06_10_2020-EX-4.5-CONSULTING AGREEMENT - DR. GAETANO MORELLO N.D. INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Consequences of Termination of Agreement. All obligations of the Company to the Contractor hereunder shall immediately terminate and cease as of the date of the termination of the Contractor's engagement. The Company shall only be obliged to pay the Fees agreed to but not yet paid as of the date of termination, with such payment to be made within 30 days of the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Consequences of Termination of Agreement. All obligations of the Company to the Contractor hereunder shall immediately terminate and cease as of the date of the termination of the Contractor's engagement. The Company shall only be obliged to pay the Fees agreed to but not yet paid as of the date of termination, with such payment to be made within 90 days of the date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the payment period for outstanding fees to 90 days after termination could contradict provincial regulations regarding timely payment of wages or fees owed to contractors.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 17 of the BC Employment Standards Act requires payment of all wages owed within a certain timeframe after termination. While this is an independent contractor agreement, delaying payment for 90 days could be construed as unreasonably delaying payment for services rendered and may conflict with the spirit, if not the strict letter, of the legislation regarding timely payment.

perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment period to 90 days, while the Law Explanation suggests this could conflict with the BC Employment Standards Act regarding timely payment of wages. Scraped Law Snippet 1 shows Section 17 of the BC Employment Standards Act deals with 'Paydays' and Section 18 addresses payment 'If employment is terminated.' The change directly alters the timeframe for payment after termination, potentially contradicting the Act's intention for timely wage payment, thus creating an inconsistency legal contradiction as defined.
